Case Study UX · Healthcare · AI 2024–25
🏆 £6.7M Pitch Win · Swiss Healthcare Hackathon · 2025

AI System for Medical Writers

Designing an AI-powered Word plugin that compresses 6–8 months of medical document writing into days — pitched and won a £6.7M contract at a Swiss healthcare hackathon.

Role
Lead UX Designer
Team of 3
Timeline
6 months
Full-time
Type
Real product pitch
Internal hackathon
Collaborators
Business, Dev
Medical Writers
£6.7M
Hackathon pitch won — business case validated
20–30%
Productivity improvement in document drafting
2 days
To first-pass document — down from 6–8 months
0
New apps writers needed to learn
01 — The Problem

Medical writing is painstakingly slow

02 — Goals

What we set out to achieve

Four measurable targets the product was designed to hit — tied directly to business outcomes the Swiss healthcare client cared about.

03 — My Role

Leading design in a cross-functional team

I defined the IA and end-to-end user flows, led wireframing and hi-fi design, made the call to pivot to a Word plugin, and ran continuous design reviews with embedded medical writers throughout the project.

04 — Key Decision

From web app to Microsoft Word plugin

Early in the project we faced a fundamental question: build a standalone platform or meet writers where they already are? I made the call to pivot to a Word add-in.

✕ Web Application
Standalone Platform
  • Significant dev overhead — rebuilding text editing from scratch
  • New tool means a steep learning curve for writers
  • Context-switching away from their core writing environment
→
✓ Word Plugin
Microsoft Word Add-in
  • All text editing capabilities available out of the box
  • Writers already live in Word — zero learning curve
  • Dramatically lower dev cost and faster time to ship
💡 This single decision saved months of development effort and made adoption near-frictionless — we could focus 100% of design effort on the AI experience.
05 — Research

Embedded medical writers as continuous research partners

Rather than one-off interviews, we had medical writers embedded in the team — every design decision was validated in real time. Three key insights shaped everything that followed.

Insight 01
Writers think in documents, not workflows
The plugin had to feel like a natural extension of their writing — not an external tool requiring context-switching.
Insight 02
Trust in AI output is fragile
Citations and source traceability are non-negotiable for regulatory confidence. Writers need to verify every AI-generated claim.
Insight 03
Prompts are intimidating
Medical writers aren't prompt engineers. We needed to guide them to write effective prompts without requiring AI literacy.
06 — Design Strategy

Three decisions that shaped the product

Decision 01
Leverage Word's native capabilities
Added a ContentCatalyst ribbon tab with AI-powered actions that felt native — no new interface to learn.
Decision 02
Make prompt-writing approachable
Guided prompt templates, a Prompt Library, and contextual hints let writers describe what they needed in plain language.
Decision 03
Two distinct roles: Admin & Author
Admins configure templates and guardrails. Authors operate within them — ensuring governance without burdening writers.
07 — The Flow

From template to first draft in one session

Alex, a Senior Medical Writer, opens a CSR template in Word and generates the first draft without ever leaving the document.

01
Open Template
Select CSR template in Word
02
Set Parameters
Drug name, study ID, sources
03
Generate Draft
AI fills all placeholders
04
Review Citations
Verify source traceability
05
Refine
Edit sections via AI prompts
08 — The Design

Design details that built writer trust

Every detail was designed around one question: will a medical writer trust this enough to submit for regulatory review?

Detail 01
ContentCatalyst ribbon tab
Familiar entry point inside Word — no new app to learn, no context switching, no friction at the start of every session.
Detail 02
Placeholder colour system
AI-generated text highlighted in blue — writers always know what's templated vs. AI vs. user-written. Transparency builds trust.
Detail 03
16 traceable citations per section
Every AI claim linked to exact source files — non-negotiable for the regulatory confidence medical writers require.
09 — Results

A product that won — and transformed a workflow

£6.7M
Hackathon pitch won — validated the business case for the product
2 days
To generate a first-pass document — down from 6–8 months
0 apps
Writers needed to learn — lived entirely inside Microsoft Word
Reflection
"The biggest challenge was designing for AI under real constraints — understanding what was feasible for our dev team while moving fast. Working under ambiguity while advocating for the user is where this project sharpened me most as a lead designer."
